claim: The zodiac section's anomalously low entropy (3.7149 bits, lowest of all sections) results from a label-only encoding where each glyph token maps to one of a small closed set of calendar or ordinal terms (month names, numerals, star names), making the zodiac section structurally independent from the prose cipher used in herbal and recipes sections.
evidence_for: Zodiac entropy 3.7149 bits is the lowest across all sections, consistent with a restricted vocabulary of labels
Zodiac section has only 1,297 words across 12 folios â€” far fewer words per folio than herbal (84/folio) or recipes (464/folio)
Label-heavy sections would naturally produce lower entropy due to repetition of a small token set
Astronomical section also shows reduced entropy (3.7471 bits), consistent with a shared label-encoding scheme
evidence_against: Low entropy could also result from heavier use of the word-initial constraint glyphs {o,c,q,s,d} rather than a label vocabulary
No confirmed correspondence between zodiac tokens and known calendar terms has been established
If zodiac uses a different cipher, Currier A/B classification within the zodiac would need to be independent â€” not yet verified
test: Compute the type-token ratio (TTR) and the frequency distribution of the top-20 word types in the zodiac section; compare TTR and top-type coverage to all other sections; test whether zodiac top-20 words account for a significantly higher fraction of total zodiac tokens than in herbal or recipes sections.
test_metric: Fraction of total zodiac tokens covered by the top-20 word types versus the same metric for herbal and recipes sections
test_threshold: Confirm if zodiac top-20 coverage > 60% of tokens AND herbal/recipes top-20 coverage < 40%; deny if zodiac top-20 coverage is within 5 percentage points of herbal top-20 coverage

tests_run.test: compute the type-token ratio (ttr) and the frequency distribution of the top-20 word types in the zo
tests_run.details: Section entropy std dev: 0.0593. Range: 3.7149 - 3.9016. Variable across sections.

reasoning: A label-only encoding would produce a sharply peaked frequency distribution with a small number of types dominating total token count; this is directly testable and, if confirmed, would justify analyzing the zodiac independently from prose sections.
